sourceMovie.title: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit (1993) as a music theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The music and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: School of Rock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit clicked for you, School of Rock (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit?
faq.answer: Try School of Rock, Pitch Perfect 2, High School Musical—each shares DNA with Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit?
faq.answer: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit (1993) is best described as Music, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sister Act Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
